response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.

persona.time_onsocialmedia: You spend a few times monthly on social media

response_parsed.opinion: Social media has changed a lot since I was young, and I’ve noticed that not everything shared online is true. I’ve always trusted my own senses and the wisdom I’ve learned from my parents—my mother, who had a strong sense of right and wrong, and my father, who taught me to question things before accepting them. Even though I don’t spend much time on social media, when I do, I try to be careful. I know that some people post things to gain attention or to spread fear, and that can be very upsetting. I’ve seen false stories about health or politics that make people anxious, and that worries me because I believe in peace and truth. I don’t follow too many accounts, and when I do, I only follow those that share facts and kindness. I also pray before I decide what to believe, because I think faith helps me stay grounded. I don’t get upset easily, but when I see something untrue, I tell myself to step back and think. I’ve learned that truth is not loud—it’s quiet, steady, and patient. That’s how I manage fake content: with a calm mind, a strong heart, and a little bit of faith in what is real.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: The persona is 74 years old, with high extraversion and high neuroticism, indicating a social but emotionally sensitive nature. Her background in a working-class family, combined with her religious beliefs and personal experiences, shapes a cautious, reflective approach to information. Given her limited social media use and high emotional sensitivity, she prioritizes mental well-being and spiritual grounding over rapid information consumption. The opinion integrates personal values, family influence, and faith to provide a grounded, human-centered perspective on managing misinformation. The tone is calm, reflective, and rooted in lived experience.
response_parsed.tone: calm, reflective, grounded in personal values and faith
